failure_summary: The parent reward generates only partial_progress trajectories; no success-like outcomes are observed. Many trajectories remain ambiguous. The reward heavily penalizes terminal states (average terminal_penalty -11.25) without providing a reliable terminal bonus, which may discourage the policy from attempting safe landings. The late_stability and flight_stability components also impose significant negative rewards, possibly making stable landing behavior costly.

main_useful_patterns: partial_progress trajectories demonstrate some approach and distance improvement
Progress-conditioned architecture appears to encourage movement toward pad
uncertainties: No failure or success-like labels available for contrast
Partial progress labels may not reliably indicate good trajectory quality
Label margin insufficient; cannot trust partial progress as strong preference evidence

component_diagnosis.evidence: Component shows positive mean on partial_progress trajectories (4.23) and aligns with progress-conditioned approach. No failure reference suggests it is not harmful.
Mean is -20.64 on partial_progress, but without failure reference cannot determine if appropriate. May be too harsh.
Mean -23.99; this is a large penalty that may discourage stable behavior near ground. Consider gating or reducing weight.
Mean is 0.71, rarely triggered; but it rewards proper landing contact. Keep but consider making it easier to obtain.
Mean -11.25, applied on most terminations. This may penalize attempts to land. Consider restricting to clear crashes only.
Mean 0.0, never triggered. The current conditions (near_pad, both legs, etc.) are likely too strict. Either gate with more lenient criteria or remove if not working.
Mean -7.38; similar to late_stability, large penalty. Consider reducing weight or gating by altitude.

forbidden_changes: do not add dense alive bonus without progress gating
do not only scale all coefficients
do not treat partial_progress as success_like
required_changes: Make terminal bonus more attainable by relaxing landing criteria (e.g., require y<0.15, low velocity, near upright, but not necessarily both legs initially)
Reduce or gate terminal_penalty to only apply on clear crash (high velocity or large angle)
Reduce magnitude of late_stability and flight_stability penalties to avoid discouraging landing attempts
Ensure early_approach and leg_bonus are preserved

rollback_decision.reason: Round 0 bootstrap provided initial progress; no clear regression. Memory is weak but not useless. Proceed with structural mutations.

self_evaluation_lesson: Without failure or success contrasts, diagnosis is cautious. Focus on enabling terminal success by reducing penalty dominance and increasing reward clarity for near-landing states. Avoid coefficient-only changes; require structural adjustments to terminal and stability components.
